name: add-clidash
tagline: Installs a read-only, zero-dependency web dashboard that turns any JSON-emitting CLI (ncl, docker, kubectl) into browsable tabs and tables.
verdict: Solid installer: read-only by construction (execFile-only, allowlisted argv, no shell), binds 127.0.0.1 by default, good troubleshooting. Weak spots: description never states when to trigger it, no config-format example, step 1's cp -R is not idempotent (nests a stale duplicate on re-run), and no version/changelog.

highlights.summary: description: Add clidash — a zero-dependency, read-only web dashboard that derives its tabs and tables... — states what it does, never when to invoke it.
| `clis.<name>.list` | argv template; `{resource}` is the only substitution | — no worked example of the argv template shown in the body.
cp -R .claude/skills/add-clidash/add/tools/clidash tools/clidash — re-running after tools/clidash exists nests a stale duplicate copy instead of updating; no existence check.
